beacons.title: Beacon: Nigeria Infrastructure Financing Gap Escalation
beacons.draft_text: Nigeria’s infrastructure financing gap remains materially above available annual public allocation capacity. The widening gap increases pressure on sovereign capital allocation authorities to approve structured, governance-aligned platforms that compress risk before FID. This strengthens the probability of mandate-based pre-FID structuring engagements. If FID boundary and downside absorption are undefined, governance jurisdiction cannot bind signatures safely. Capital stack sequencing must be defined before commitment can harden.

beacons.title: Beacon: Nigeria reserve rebuild improves commitment conditions
beacons.mandate_implication: Macro-buffer strengthening improves perceived FX stability and supports tighter pre-FID mandate conversion windows for sovereign/DFI capital.
beacons.draft_text: Nigeria's reported reserve rebuild materially changes commitment physics. A stronger external buffer and transparency signal reduce perceived convertibility and volatility risk, which can compress hesitation in pre-FID decision cycles. The mandate path should now emphasize timing: move from broad positioning to structured conversion steps with defined governance and downside controls while macro confidence is rising.

beacons.title: Beacon: CLARITY Act shifts U.S. digital-asset commitment architecture
beacons.mandate_implication: Regulatory boundary clarity and stronger enforcement rails can unlock compliant infrastructure mandates by reducing legal ambiguity at commitment stage.
beacons.draft_text: The CLARITY Act framing indicates a structural shift from enforcement ambiguity toward rules-based jurisdiction and compliance expectations. If enacted on this trajectory, commitment conditions improve for regulated digital-asset infrastructure because legal boundary risk becomes more definable. The immediate move is to position mandate pathways around compliant onshore architecture, explicit control-point governance, and investor-protection alignment, rather than speculative token narratives.

beacons.title: Beacon: India AI factory scale-up raises infrastructure urgency threshold
beacons.mandate_implication: AI infrastructure commitments are moving toward sovereign-scale execution; mandate framing should emphasize speed, power density planning, and governance-defined capital sequencing.
beacons.draft_text: The L&T–NVIDIA announcement indicates that sovereign-scale AI infrastructure is moving from narrative to execution. This raises the urgency threshold for platforms that still treat compute infrastructure as optional or deferred. Commitment architecture should now prioritize power-secure deployment, phased capacity milestones, and governance clarity that allows capital to move before strategic windows close.
